source_old_req: The controller component shall define the boolean variables heating_request and cooling_request. The heating_request shall be true if and only if the user_request contains a heating command and does not contain a cooling command. Likewise, the cooling_request shall be true if and only if the user_request contains a cooling command and does not contain a heating command. Additionally, the controller shall ensure through assertions that: the heater_on output is true only if heating_request is true and the tempin input is less than or equal to 20 degrees; the cooler_on output is true only if cooling_request is true and the tempin input is greater than 0 degrees.

expected_check.note: Existing requirement kept because it is explicit and non-vague.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

The heating_request shall be true if and only if the user_request contains a heating command and does not contain a cooling command. Likewise, the cooling_request shall be true if and only if the user_request contains a cooling command and does not contain a heating command. behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

The heating_request shall be true if and only if the user_request contains a heating command and does not contain a cooling command. Likewise, the cooling_request shall be true if and only if the user_request contains a cooling command and does not contain a heating command. paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
